the 'best' option assumes the pitcher has good slider command, which may not apply to all age groups despite diff:3 rating

worstScenarios.title: The Pine Tar Game
issues: conceptTag 'force-vs-tag' is completely wrong for a rules interpretation scenario about pine tar
Position 'famous' is not a valid baseball position - should be 'manager' since this is a managerial decision
Teaching 6-year-olds about a 1983 pine tar technicality rule is not age-appropriate for diff:1
This scenario doesn't teach applicable baseball strategy - it's just historical trivia
The concept has nothing to do with the force-vs-tag principle
strengths: Historically accurate recreation of the famous Pine Tar Game incident
Good explanation of why the AL president overturned the original ruling
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Replace with an actual force-vs-tag play scenario for the appropriate position, or change conceptTag to 'rules-interpretation' and position to 'manager' while increasing difficulty to 2 or 3 for older kids who can appreciate historical rule controversies

worstScenarios.title: Infield Fly Rule
issues: conceptTag 'double-play-turn' doesn't match the scenario which teaches infield fly rule
Option 3 explanation is incomplete - doesn't explain WHY the rule exists or what happens
Explanations don't teach the CONDITIONS for infield fly (fair territory, ordinary effort, runners on 1st/2nd or bases loaded)
Category 'rules' and position 'rules' are redundant - should use a specific fielding position
Rates don't match realistic confusion patterns - option 3 (25%) should be higher than option 1 (20%)
strengths: Accurately explains the core infield fly rule mechanics
Good use of a common game situation where this rule applies
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change position to 'umpire' or specific fielder, fix conceptTag to 'infield-fly-rule', expand explanations to teach WHEN the rule applies (fair ball, ordinary effort, force play possible at 3rd), and adjust rates so dead ball option (20%) has lower rate than must-catch option (25%)

worstScenarios.title: Designated Hitter Rule
issues: conceptTag 'force-vs-tag' is completely wrong — this is about DH rules, not force plays
Position 'rules' doesn't match standard positions (should be 'manager')
Explanations are repetitive — all say 'DH bats for pitcher' without teaching different aspects
Description lacks game context — why is manager deciding this in Top 1 with game started?
No explanation teaches WHY the DH rule exists or its strategic implications
strengths: Factually accurate about 2022 universal DH rule
Age-appropriate language for diff:1
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change position to 'manager', conceptTag to 'dh-rule', and rewrite explanations to teach: 1) What DH is, 2) Why it helps (pitchers focus on pitching), 3) Why pinch hitting fails (removes pitcher), 4) Why double-batting is illegal

worstScenarios.title: Runner on Second — Check Before Throwing
issues: Best answer is option 2 (tag at third) but has rate of 25, while option 2 (look back then throw) has rate of 85 - rates don't match best designation
Looking the runner back to 2nd before throwing to first is the correct play here, not attempting a tag at third on a non-force play
Explanation for option 2 correctly identifies no force play but doesn't explain why this makes it a poor choice
Rates don't follow required distribution - best option should be 65-90 but is only 25
strengths: Realistic game situation that teaches important concept about checking runners
Age-appropriate language and complexity for diff:1
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change best answer to option 2 (look runner back) and swap rates between options 1 and 2. Update explanation for option 1 to explain why tagging at third on a non-force is rarely successful.

worstScenarios.title: Pop Fly Priority
issues: Explanation 3 answers for option 3 correctly, but explanation 4 contradicts the scenario by telling RF to back off for CF when the player IS the CF
Score shows home team leading 1-0 in top of 2nd, but description doesn't establish meaningful game pressure to justify diff:1
Best option rate of 90 is too high for a communication scenario where multiple valid approaches exist
Option 1 could be more specific about when to call it (immediately vs after assessing)
strengths: Teaches critical safety concept of CF priority to prevent collisions
Age-appropriate language and concept for 6+ year olds
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Fix explanation 4 to explain why RF should back off (e.g., 'RF should yield because you have the better angle from CF'), and lower best option rate to 75-80 to reflect that timing of the call matters

worstScenarios.title: Intentional Walk
issues: Description says '.220 hitter (8 HRs)' but explanation says '.245 hitter' - inconsistent stats
ConceptTag 'force-vs-tag' doesn't match the actual concept being taught (intentional walk strategy)
Explanation 0 incorrectly states walking sets up force at EVERY base - only at 3rd and home, not 2nd
Option 3 'Walk both' is unclear - can't intentionally walk the on-deck hitter
Explanation 3 about 'cold reliever' doesn't connect to the strategic decision of bringing in a new pitcher
strengths: Realistic late-game pressure situation that happens frequently in baseball
Good use of batting averages to create a clear strategic decision
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Fix the batting average inconsistency (.220 vs .245), change conceptTag to 'intentional-walk', clarify option 3 to 'Walk him, then pitch carefully to next batter', and rewrite explanations to be more specific about the strategic reasoning

worstScenarios.title: Two-Two Tightrope
issues: Concept tag 'pickoff-mechanics' doesn't match scenario content about pitch selection and strikeouts
No indication how a 9-year-old pitcher would know slider whiff rates (30%) or have 4 different pitches
Score perspective error: Top 7th means visiting team bats, so 'up 5-4' should be 'down 5-4' from pitcher's view
Curveball explanation focuses on wild pitch risk rather than strategic choice evaluation
Rates don't reflect realistic strikeout probabilities for youth baseball
strengths: Clear strategic dilemma between strikeout vs contact in high-leverage situation
Explanations teach valuable principle about preventing scoring with runner on third
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change conceptTag to 'pitch-selection', fix score perspective (down 4-5 in Top 7th), simplify to 2-3 pitch options appropriate for youth level, remove advanced analytics references

worstScenarios.title: Baez's Rundown Magic
issues: Runner on 1st only but explanation mentions 'other runner score' - factual error
Best answer (option 3) references non-existent baserunner
Scenario setup contradicts the actual play - Baez's famous rundown had a runner on 3rd
Explanations don't clearly differentiate why option 3 is better than option 4
Category 'famous' doesn't match standard positions - should be 'baserunner'
strengths: Creative use of real MLB moment to teach strategy
Engaging scenario that kids would find exciting
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change runners to [1,3] to match the actual Baez play, rewrite option 3 as 'Create chaos to help runner on 3rd score', and file under 'baserunner' position instead of 'famous'

worstScenarios.title: First Baseman's DP — Bases Loaded
issues: Factual error: With bases loaded and 1 out, stepping on first creates out #2, but throwing home is NOT a force play anymore - the force is removed once first base is touched
The 'best' answer (step on first, throw home) would not work as described - catcher cannot get a force out at home after first base is touched
Explanation states 'catcher steps on home plate for the force out' which is incorrect - it would require a tag after stepping on first
The correct play would be throw home first for force, then to first for double play (option 2 is actually closest to correct)
strengths: Realistic game situation with clear strategic decision
Good difficulty level for ages 12+ with advanced double play concept
worstScenarios.suggestedFix: Change best answer to option 2 (throw home first, then cover first) and rewrite explanations to correctly explain that stepping on first removes the force at home, so home-first preserves both force outs

results.title: Full Count: 3-2 with 2 Outs
issues: The scenario description says runner on 1st, but being down 3-4 in the 7th with 2 outs makes driving in that runner impossible from 1st base
Explanation 2 incorrectly states 'contact puts him in scoring position' - runner on 1st goes to 2nd on a single, not scoring position
The concept of 'runner going on the pitch' is underutilized in the explanations - only option 2 mentions it briefly
Rate for option 0 (30) seems low for a common youth baseball approach of protecting the plate with 2 strikes
strengths: Excellent teaching of 3-2 count discipline - being selective even with 2 strikes is advanced strategy
Options present realistic approaches players actually debate in this situation
results.suggestedFix: Fix the factual error in explanation 2: change 'puts him in scoring position' to 'moves him into scoring position at 2nd base' and better incorporate how the runner stealing affects the batter's approach

results.title: Hit and Run Coverage
issues: Explanation for option 1 could emphasize more that this is the standard defensive assignment on hit-and-run plays
strengths: Excellent teaching of hit-and-run defensive responsibilities
Clearly explains why 2B holds position while SS covers the bag
Age-appropriate complexity for diff:3

results.title: The 5-4-3 Double Play — Your Throw
issues: Situation shows outs as 1 but options discuss getting 'the out at second' as if no one is out yet
Option 2 explanation mentions 'force' but with bases loaded and 1 out, it's already a force at every base
strengths: Excellent use of MLB data to support the teaching point
Clear concept that accuracy beats speed on double play pivots
Realistic game pressure with bases loaded in a close game

results.title: Two-Two Tightrope
issues: Concept tag 'pickoff-mechanics' doesn't match scenario content about pitch selection and strikeouts
No indication how a 9-year-old pitcher would know slider whiff rates (30%) or have 4 different pitches
Score perspective error: Top 7th means visiting team bats, so 'up 5-4' should be 'down 5-4' from pitcher's view
Curveball explanation focuses on wild pitch risk rather than strategic choice evaluation
Rates don't reflect realistic strikeout probabilities for youth baseball
strengths: Clear strategic dilemma between strikeout vs contact in high-leverage situation
Explanations teach valuable principle about preventing scoring with runner on third
results.suggestedFix: Change conceptTag to 'pitch-selection', fix score perspective (down 4-5 in Top 7th), simplify to 2-3 pitch options appropriate for youth level, remove advanced analytics references

results.title: Infield In — Fly or Ground?
issues: Rates don't quite match realistic probabilities - 85% success rate for lifting a fly ball is too high for most youth players
Could better explain WHY infields play in (to get force at home) in the explanations
strengths: Excellent teaching of a specific strategic concept that many young players don't understand
Perfect game situation setup that clearly demonstrates when to apply this principle
Explanations each teach different aspects without repetition

results.title: Baez's Rundown Magic
issues: Runner on 1st only but explanation mentions 'other runner score' - factual error
Best answer (option 3) references non-existent baserunner
Scenario setup contradicts the actual play - Baez's famous rundown had a runner on 3rd
Explanations don't clearly differentiate why option 3 is better than option 4
Category 'famous' doesn't match standard positions - should be 'baserunner'
strengths: Creative use of real MLB moment to teach strategy
Engaging scenario that kids would find exciting
results.suggestedFix: Change runners to [1,3] to match the actual Baez play, rewrite option 3 as 'Create chaos to help runner on 3rd score', and file under 'baserunner' position instead of 'famous'
